name: Parkway Bank And Trust Company
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank headquartered in Chicago since 1964, offering personal and commercial banking with branches in Chicago and Phoenix.
description_long:
Parkway Bank and Trust Company has operated as a community-focused financial institution in the Chicago area since 1964. The bank has expanded to serve customers in both Chicago and Phoenix, positioning itself as a traditional community bank rather than a large national institution. The organization emphasizes local relationships and personalized service through what they describe as "what a community bank should be."

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including free checking accounts (Kasasa Cash, Kasasa Cash Back, Totally Free Checking, Senior Citizen Checking), savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, IRAs, and debit cards. For borrowing needs, they provide personal loans, home mortgage loans, home equity loans, and auto loans. On the commercial side, Parkway Bank offers business checking, business savings, commercial NOW accounts, business loans, SBA loans, commercial real estate financing, equipment loans, and franchise lending. They also provide treasury management services, merchant processing, and receivables/payables solutions for business customers.

Parkway Bank distinguishes itself through membership in FDIC protection, backed by U.S. government guarantee. They offer competitive CD rates (3.78% APY for 5-month terms as of their latest marketing materials) and Kasasa-branded products with rates up to 2.65% APY on checking. The bank maintains a local decision-making approach and has emphasized their experienced team of local bankers. They provide digital banking options including online banking, mobile banking, mobile check deposit, and Zelle for both personal and business customers.

As a regional community bank, Parkway Bank serves customers seeking traditional banking relationships with personal service. However, customers should note that as a smaller regional institution, they may have fewer branch locations and potentially different rate competitiveness compared to national banks. The bank is FDIC-insured, which provides standard deposit protection up to $250,000 per account category. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal checking accounts (Free Kasasa Cash, Free Kasasa Cash Back, Totally Free Checking, Senior Citizen Checking)
Savings accounts and money market accounts
Home mortgage loans and home equity loans
Business checking and commercial checking accounts
Business loans, SBA loans, and commercial real estate loans
Mobile banking and online bill pay
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full U.S. government backing
Competitive CD rates at 3.78% APY for 5-month terms
Free checking account options including Kasasa Cash with up to 2.65% APY
Comprehensive commercial banking with SBA lending and treasury management services
Mobile banking and check deposit capabilities
Senior Citizen Checking account specifically designed for older customers
Zelle integration for both personal and business customers
cons: Limited geographic presence (only Chicago and Phoenix areas) compared to national banks
Smaller institution may have fewer ATM locations despite claiming 'free ATMs'
No mention of investment services, brokerage, or wealth management offerings
Limited information on loan terms, rates, or APR ranges on personal/business loans
Regional bank may have higher minimum balance requirements than larger competitors

contact.address: 4800 N Harlem Ave, Harwood Heights, IL 60706

best_for: Chicago and Phoenix area residents seeking community-focused banking with personal relationships
Small to mid-market businesses needing customized commercial banking and SBA loan options
Customers prioritizing FDIC deposit insurance and local decision-making over national brand recognition
Senior citizens looking for specialized checking products designed for their needs
diagnosis: Parkway Bank is best suited for individuals and businesses in the Chicago and Phoenix areas who value community banking relationships, personalized service, and FDIC-insured deposits. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this is a regional bank without national presence, so customers outside Chicago and Phoenix cannot access their services, and those within these regions may have fewer branches and ATMs than larger national competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 4800 N Harlem Ave, Harwood Heights, IL 60706
